element_choice.justification: Every element is semantically precise: <article> for main and card content, <time datetime> for all dates, <pre><code> for the code block, <aside> (not <blockquote>) for the pull quote, <address> correctly scoped to author contact links, <dl> for key-value metadata, <strong> for comment author names, and native form elements throughout. No div-for-semantic-element substitutions.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used only where needed: aria-label on unlabelled landmarks (nav, aside, footer nav), aria-labelledby on sections and articles, aria-describedby linking the email hint, aria-hidden on decorative asterisks, and tabindex=-1 on the skip-link target. No redundant role attributes or ARIA compensating for non-semantic markup.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 for the article title; h2 for all major sections (The Problem, Our Approach, Results, About the Author, Related Articles, Comments); h3 for Leave a Comment (subsection of Comments) and for related article card titles (subsection of Related Articles). No skipped levels, no multiple h1s.

landmark_structure.justification: <main> correctly wraps the article, related articles, and comments section while the site header and footer remain outside it. All nav elements are aria-labelled, all sections have aria-labelledby, the pull quote uses <aside>, and two appropriate skip links (to main content and to comments) appear as the first focusable elements.

form_semantics.justification: All three inputs (text, email, textarea) have explicit <label> elements. Appropriate type attributes (type=email) and autocomplete attributes (name, email) are present. The email hint is linked via aria-describedby. Required fields carry both the required attribute and a visible asterisk indicator. <button type=submit> used correctly.

content_realism.justification: All content is fully realistic: a detailed narrative article about a real technology migration, a working DataLoader code sample, specific metrics (4.3 to 1.1 requests, 62% payload reduction), realistic commenter names and substantive comments, and plausible related article titles and authors. No lorem ipsum or placeholder text anywhere.

list_semantics.justification: Nav items use <ul> within <nav>, related article cards are wrapped in <ul> (unordered collection), and comments are wrapped in <ol> (chronologically ordered). Footer nav also uses <ul>. List types are correctly matched to content semantics.

expectations_met: <main> wraps the article, related articles, and comments — site header and footer are outside it
Skip navigation links are the first focusable elements ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to comments')
Article date uses <time> with a datetime attribute (datetime='2025-11-12')
Article sections use h2 headings within the article
Related articles section uses an h2 heading and article cards in a <ul>
Comments are each an <article> (self-contained, in an <ol>)
Comment form uses proper <label> elements for all fields
Comment author names use <strong>, dates use <time>
<address> used correctly for author contact links only
Heading hierarchy is consistent: h1 for article title, h2 for sections, related articles, and comments headings
additional_observations: <dl> used for article metadata (author, published date, topics) — a well-chosen key-value structure
Comments in <ol> rather than <ul> reflects their chronological ordering — a thoughtful choice
tabindex=-1 on <main> correctly supports programmatic focus from the skip link
aria-labelledby on <article> elements for comments references <strong> author names — valid and useful for screen reader navigation
